mention_a.name: Gradient
source_spans: The gradient of a differentiable scalar function is the vector of its partial derivatives.

mention_b.name: Gradient Vector
source_spans: The gradient vector points in the direction of steepest local increase of a differentiable objective.

mention_a.name: Likelihood
source_spans: The likelihood is viewed as a function of the model parameters with the sample held fixed.

mention_b.name: Likelihood Function
source_spans: The likelihood function assigns each candidate parameter value a score determined by the fixed observations.

mention_a.name: Likelihood
source_spans: The likelihood is viewed as a function of the model parameters with the sample held fixed.

mention_b.name: Likelihood
source_spans: The likelihood compares parameter values after the observed data are held fixed.

mention_a.name: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation
source_spans: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation estimates an econometric model by maximizing the likelihood of the observed sample.

mention_b.name: MLE
source_spans: MLE is used to fit a probabilistic model by maximizing its likelihood function on observed training data.

mention_a.name: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation
source_spans: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation estimates an econometric model by maximizing the likelihood of the observed sample.

mention_b.name: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
source_spans: Maximum Likelihood Estimation selects parameter values that maximize the likelihood.

mention_a.name: Degree
source_spans: The degree of a vertex is the number of edges incident to that vertex in an undirected graph.

mention_b.name: Degree
source_spans: The degree of a nonzero polynomial is the largest exponent whose coefficient is nonzero.

mention_a.name: Likelihood Function
source_spans: The likelihood function assigns each candidate parameter value a score determined by the fixed observations.

mention_b.name: Likelihood
source_spans: The likelihood compares parameter values after the observed data are held fixed.

mention_a.name: MLE
source_spans: MLE is used to fit a probabilistic model by maximizing its likelihood function on observed training data.

mention_b.name: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
source_spans: Maximum Likelihood Estimation selects parameter values that maximize the likelihood.

mention_a.name: Euler's Method
source_spans: Euler's Method approximates its solution by repeatedly applying

mention_b.name: Forward Euler Method
source_spans: The Forward Euler Method advances an approximate solution using the current slope.

mention_a.name: ODE
source_spans: An ODE describes a function through derivatives with respect to one independent variable.

mention_b.name: Ordinary Differential Equation
source_spans: An ordinary differential equation relates an unknown function of one variable to its derivatives.

mention_a.name: $y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_n,y_n)$
source_spans: y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_n,y_n)

mention_b.name: Forward Euler Update
source_spans: y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_n,y_n)
